# Extended Pattern Matching

* [Documentation](https://matchor.readthedocs.io/en/stable/)
* [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/matchor/)
* [Sources](https://github.com/nbiotcloud/matchor)
* [Issues](https://github.com/nbiotcloud/matchor/issues)

## Installation

Installing it is pretty easy:

```bash
pip install matchor
```

functions.name: matchor.is_pattern
documentation: Return `True` if `pattern` contains `*` or `?`.

functions.name: matchor.match
documentation:
Return `True` if `name` matches `pattern`.

Pattern may contain `*` and `?`. `*` matches any characters, `?` matches exactly one character.

```pycon
>>> match('foo', 'foo')
True
>>> match('foo', '*')
True
>>> match('foo', 'bar*')
False
>>> match('foo', 'fo?')
True
```

functions.name: matchor.matchs
documentation:
Return `True` if `name` matches element of `patterns`.

Pattern may contain `*` and `?`. `*` matches any characters, `?` matches exactly one character.

```pycon
>>> matchs('foo', ['foo', 'bar'])
True
>>> matchs('foo', ['*'])
True
>>> matchs('foo', ['bar*', 'baz'])
False
>>> matchs('ba', ['bar*', 'baz'])
False
>>> matchs('baz', ['bar*', 'baz'])
True
>>> matchs('baz2', ['bar*', 'baz'])
False
```

functions.name: matchor.matchsp
documentation:
Return matching pattern if `name` matches element of `patterns`.

Pattern may contain `*` and `?`. `*` matches any characters, `?` matches exactly one character.

```pycon
>>> matchsp('foo', ['foo', 'bar'])
'foo'
>>> matchsp('foo', ['*'])
'*'
>>> matchsp('foo', ['bar*', 'baz'])
>>> matchsp('ba', ['bar*', 'baz'])
>>> matchsp('baz', ['bar*', 'baz'])
'baz'
>>> matchsp('baz2', ['bar*', 'baz'])
```
